_comment: Workshop Video 01 — The Setup. Distilled from Intro chapters 1-4 + Module 1. Single-stem rendering: scenes joined with break tags, one ElevenLabs call. Visual concerns (clipDurationSeconds, label, title) live in src/WorkshopVideo01TheSetup.tsx. House voice preset.

scenes.text: Start in VS Code. Open the marketplace, search for Claude Code, install the extension from Anthropic, then reload the window. That's the whole install.
Now the loop. You prompt Claude. Claude acts on the code. You check what changed. You prompt again. Every workflow you build lives inside that one loop.
Pick a theme once and it stays. Dracula, GitHub Light, Night Owl — whatever you like. VS Code remembers it across every project.
Command-Shift-P opens the command palette — your shortcut to everything. Don't want to learn the settings file? Ask Claude to write your config for you.
One file at the project root: CLAUDE-dot-M-D. Claude reads it every turn. It's where your project's brain lives — rules, paths, intent. Build this file slowly, and Claude gets sharper every session.
